question.text: The Black-Scholes model uses which five key determinants to calculate option price?

answer.text: Underlying price, strike price, volatility, time to expiration, and risk-free interest rate
Underlying price, lot size, tick size, margin, and expiry date
Open interest, volume, volatility, delta, and gamma
Spot price, futures price, storage cost, convenience yield, and carry cost

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The five inputs to Black-Scholes are: (1) underlying price, (2) strike price, (3) volatility, (4) time to expiration, and (5) short-term risk-free interest rate. **The Trap:** Lot size, tick size, and margin are contract specifications, not pricing inputs. Greeks are outputs, not inputs. Cost-of-carry factors are for futures pricing, not options. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII and I use the same five-parameter framework. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Among the five inputs, volatility is the only one not directly observable — it must be estimated. This is why implied volatility (derived from actual market prices) is so important.
